name: openepd

metadata.name: openepd
classifiers: License :: OSI Approved :: Apache Software License
Topic :: Software Development :: Libraries :: Python Modules
metadata.summary: Python library to work with OpenEPD format

documentation:
# Python Library to work with OpenEPD format


This library is a Python library to work with OpenEPD format. ## About OpenEPD [openEPD](https://www.buildingtransparency.org/programs/openepd/) is an open data format for passing digital third-party verified Environmental Product Declarations (EPDs) among Program Operators, EPD Databases, Life Cycle Analysis tools, design tools, reporting, and procurement. Unlike print or PDF EPDs, openEPD provides a shared and precise format to express and refer to EPDs in ways that modern databases can use. openEPD can be used alongside a printable document, or can generate printable EPDs. Unlike existing formats such as ILCD+EPD, it enforces a key set of guarantees for interoperable data processing, including uniqueness of organizations/plants, precise PCR references, and dated version control. The openEPD format is **extensible**. Standard extensions exist for concrete products, and for documenting supply-chain specific data. [Read More about OpenEPD format here](https://www.buildingtransparency.org/programs/openepd/). ## Usage **❗ ATTENTION**: Pick the right version. The cornerstone of this library models package representing openEPD models. Models are defined with Pydantic library which is a dependency for openepd package. If you use Pydantic in your project carefully pick the version: * Use version **below** `2.0.0` if your project uses Pydantic version below `2.0.0` * Use version `2.x.x` or higher if your project uses Pydantic version `2.0.0` or above ### Models The library provides the Pydantic models for all the OpenEPD entities. The models are available in the `openepd.models` module. For mode details on the usage please refer to Pydantic documentation. ### API Client The library provides the API client to work with the OpenEPD API. The client is available in the `openepd.client` module. Currently, the only available implementation is based on synchronous [requests]() library. Client provides the following features: * Error handling - depending on HTTP status code the client raises different exceptions allowing to handle errors in a more granular way. * Throttling - the client is able to throttle the requests to the API to avoid hitting the rate limits. * Retry - the client is able to retry the requests in case of the network errors. #### API Client Usage The following example illustrates the usage of the API client: ```python from openepd.api.sync_client import OpenEpdApiClientSync # Setup the client api_client = OpenEpdApiClientSync( "https://openepd.buildingtransparency.org/api", "", ) # Use API, e.g. get EPD by ID epd = api_client.epds.get_by_openxpd_uuid("ec3b9j5t") ``` ### Bundle Bundle is a format which allows to bundle multiple openEPD objects together (it might be EPDs, PCRs, Orgs + any other files which might be related to mentioned objects like pdf version of EPD, logo of the PCR company, etc). Bundle consists of root-level and dependent objects. Dependents are objects which are referenced by root-level objects or related to the root-level objects. For example, EPD is a root-level object, PDF version of this EPD is a dependent, translated version of the same EPD is dependent as well. The following example illustrates reading of the bundle file containing PCR and some of the related objects: ```python from openepd.bundle.reader import DefaultBundleReader from openepd.bundle.model import AssetType, RelType with DefaultBundleReader("test-bundle.epb") as reader: # You could either file path or file-like object pcr = reader.get_first_root_asset(AssetType.Pcr) # Get FIRST available root level PCR object. We consider that # there is only one PCR in the bundle # Read relative PDF file of the found PCR. `related_pdf` is a reference to the PDF file containing metadata only related_pdf = reader.get_first_relative_asset(pcr, RelType.Pdf) # We have to read the file content separately with reader.read_blob_asset(related_pdf) as f: pass # Do something with the file content here ``` The next example illustrates the writing of the bundle file: ```python from openepd.bundle.writer import DefaultBundleWriter from openepd.bundle.model import RelType from openepd.model.pcr import Pcr pcr_obj = Pcr(...) # Let's assume we already have PCR object with DefaultBundleWriter("my-bundle.epb") as writer, open("test-pcr.pdf", "rb") as pcr_pdf_file: # Add our PCR to the bundle. We do not specify any extra information, however you might what to add language # and a file name to make it look nicer in the bundle. If omitted the name will be generated automatically. pcr_asset = writer.write_object_asset(pcr_obj) # Now add related PDF document. We have to specify the content type, related (parent) object and the # type of relation. Again, optionally you might want to specify the language and file name. writer.write_blob_asset(pcr_pdf_file, "application/pdf", pcr_asset, RelType.Pdf) ``` ### Model attribute access OpenEPD extends its pydantic models with extra functionality: field descriptors can be accessed via dot notation from class name: * Usual pydantic way: TheModel().__field__["the_field"] * In openEPD: TheModel.the_field Instances hold data as usual. This behaviour is enabled by default. To disable, run the code with `OPENEPD_DISABLE_PYDANTIC_PATCH` set to `true`. See src/openepd/patch_pydantic.py for details. ### Generated enums The geography and country enums are generated from several sources, including pycountry list of 2-character country codes, UN m49 codification, and special regions. To update the enums, first update any of these sources, then use `make codegen`. See 'tools/openepd/codegen' for details. ## Development Windows is not supported for development. You can use WSL2 with Ubuntu 20.04 or higher. Instructions are the same as for regular GNU/Linux installation. ### Commit messages Commit messages should follow [Conventional Commit](https://www.conventionalcommits.org/en/v1.0.0/#specification) specification as we use automatic version with [commitizen](https://commitizen-tools.github.io/commitizen/). # Credits This library has been written and maintained by [C-Change Labs](https://c-change-labs.com/). # License This library is licensed under [Apache 2](/LICENSE). This means you are free to use it in commercial projects.

```
param value

parameter value

return

encoded value
```

functions.name: openepd.api.utils.remove_none_id_fields
documentation:
Remove any key 'id' with a None value from the dictionary, including nested dicts.

```
param d

the dict which may contain 'id' keys with None values.

return

a new dict with 'id' keys that have None values removed.

note

This function does not modify the original dictionary (no side effects).

It returns a new dictionary with the necessary modifications applied.

example

>>> data = {
...     "id": None,
...     "name": "item1",
...     "details": {
...         "id": None,
...         "category": "tools",
...         "nested": {
...             "id": None,
...             "value": 42
...         }
...     }
... }
>>> remove_none_id_fields(data)
{'name': 'item1', 'details': {'category': 'tools', 'nested': {'value': 42}}}
```

methods.name: openepd.api.epd.sync_api.EpdApi.find_raw
documentation:
Find EPDs by Open Material Filter(OMF).

OMF is a query language for searching materials/EPDs, running statistics and so on.
It can exist in a string form, which is accepted by most of the endpoints.

```
param omf

OMF - open material filter string (see OMF spec).

param page_num

page number

param page_size

page size

return

the list of EPDs
```

methods.name: openepd.api.epd.sync_api.EpdApi.find
documentation:
Find EPDs by Open Material Filter(OMF).

OMF is a query language for searching materials/EPDs, running statistics and so on.
It can exist in a string form, which is accepted by most of the endpoints.

```
param omf

OMF - open material filter string (see OMF spec).

param page_size

page size, None for default

return

streaming list of EPDs
```

methods.name: openepd.api.epd.sync_api.EpdApi.get_statistics_raw
documentation:
Get statistics for a given search query.

Statistics contains aggregated parameters such as percentiles distributions of GWP and other parameters.
Please note - in addition to product EPDs statistics might include industry-wide EPDs and even generic
estimates to provide a more complete picture where there is not enough product EPDs.

```
param omf

OMF - open material filter string (see OMF spec).

return

statistics wrapped in OpenEpdApiResponse
```

methods.name: openepd.api.epd.sync_api.EpdApi.get_statistics
documentation:
Get statistics for a given search query.

Statistics contains aggregated parameters such as percentiles distributions of GWP and other parameters.
Please note - in addition to product EPDs statistics might include industry-wide EPDs and even generic
estimates to provide a more complete picture where there is not enough product EPDs.

classes.name: openepd.api.dto.common.MetaCollectionDto
documentation:
This class is intended to be used as a container for different meta objects.

From a specific controller, you should return a specific subclass of MetaCollectionDto and appropriate mixins. This
would allow to retain type information to generate schema for meta section of response.

For example, EPD Search method, which should return some metadata about performance, material filter, and paging,
would return a concrete subclass of MetaCollectionDto like this one:

`class EpdSearchViewMeta(MaterialFilterMetaMixin, PagingMetaMixin, WarningMetaMixin, MetaCollectionDto)`

Mixins are used to define the resulting meta's dictionary-like structure, this would result in a json like:
```
{
"paging": {"page_size": 100, "total_count": 1, "total_pages": 1},
"ec3_warnings": null,
"material_filter": {...}
}
```

It is generified by the extension type of similar structure (key-dict value), which should not be a part of OpenEPD
spec. This allows to add custom meta objects to the response, which would be ignored by OpenEPD implementors.

documentation:
Go to the given page and return items as result.

This will query remote server to retrieve given page, the state of iterator will be updated accordingly.
All information from the internal buffer will be overridden.

```

page number to retrieve

param force_reload

if True, force reload of the page even if it was already loaded

return

list of items on the page
```

documentation:
Iterate over all items, when needed the new pages from server will be requested.

```
param start_from_page

page number to start from (1-based)
```

methods.name: openepd.api.common.StreamingListResponse._is_initialized
documentation: Check if object is initialized (at least one page was retrieved).

methods.name: openepd.api.common.StreamingListResponse._ensure_initialized
documentation: Ensure that object is initialized, if not - request the first page.

Iterator over a list of objects which could be from remote API in batches by given fetch function.

This class could work only with OpenEpdApiResponse which:
a) contains a list of objects in payload
b) contains PagingMeta in meta

Typical use case:

    stream: StreamingListResponse[Epd] # Assume we collected this from somewhere (e.g. from API Client)

    # Iterate over all EPDs (this will fetch the next page when needed, one single page is buffered)
    for epd in stream:
        print(epd)
        print("Current page: ", stream.current_page)

    # Get specific page
    page = stream.goto_page(4)

    # Get paging information
    stream.get_paging_meta()
    # or
    stream.get_get_total_pages(), stream.get_total_items()

    # Iterate starting from specific page
    for epd in stream.iterator(4):
        print(epd)
    # Alternative approach
    stream.goto_page(4)
    for epd in stream:
        print(epd)

class_variables.name: openepd.model.declaration.OriginalDataFormat.OPENEPD_1_0
documentation:
Data was generated in an openEPD-compliant system, such as by manual entry in EC3 or generation in an openEPD
compliant EPD generator.

class_variables.name: openepd.model.declaration.OriginalDataFormat.CUSTOM_API
documentation:
Data was gathered from a non-standardized API offered by a data provider.  Numerical and text data is very likely
to be correct and up to date, but important fields may be absent and identification of organizations, plants, and
 addresses may be ambiguous.

class_variables.name: openepd.model.declaration.OriginalDataFormat.ILCD_EPD_v1_2
documentation:
Data was generated and published in ILCD+EPD format, version 1.2  Numerical and text data is very likely to be
correct and up to date, but important fields may be absent and identification of organizations, plants, and
addresses may be ambiguous.

class_variables.name: openepd.model.declaration.OriginalDataFormat.ILCD_EPD_v1_1
documentation:
Data was generated and published in ILCD+EPD format, version 1.1  Numerical and text data is very likely to be
correct and up to date, but important fields may be absent and identification of organizations, plants, and
addresses may be ambiguous.

class_variables.name: openepd.model.declaration.OriginalDataFormat.ILCD_EPD_v1_0
documentation:
Data was generated and published in ILCD+EPD format, version 1.0  Numerical and text data is very likely to be
correct and up to date, but important fields may be absent and identification of organizations, plants, and
addresses may be ambiguous.

class_variables.name: openepd.model.declaration.OriginalDataFormat.WEB
documentation:
Data was gathered through comprehension of a viewable HTML page, typically offered by a program operator.
This is more reliable than PDF, but less reliable than API or a standardized XML like ILCD+EPD.

class_variables.name: openepd.model.declaration.OriginalDataFormat.PDF_MANUAL
documentation: Data was entered by hand based on PDF and/or validated by a qualified professional.

class_variables.name: openepd.model.declaration.OriginalDataFormat.PDF_MIXED
documentation:
Data is a mix of structured data from an information system, but with significant missing data filled in by
extraction from a PDF.

class_variables.name: openepd.model.declaration.OriginalDataFormat.PDF
documentation:
Data was extracted by analyzing or comprehending a PDF document.  Includes documents where up to 4 fields (e.g.
program operator) were inferred from sources outside the PDF.  This method is prone to errors and omissions due
to the many different formats and terms used in these relatively unstructured documents.

class_variables.name: openepd.model.declaration.OriginalDataFormat.OTHER
documentation: Data source is not of a type listed here.

Original data format for this EPD.

A system receiving an EPD via openEPD should preserve this field if it exists.  Otherwise, it is up to the
receiving system to infer the original data format based on the source.

classes.name: openepd.model.common.Distribution
documentation:
Distribution of the measured value.

 * log-normal: Probability distribution of any random parameter whose natural log is normally distributed (the
   PDF is gaussian).
 * normal: Probability distribution of any random parameter whose value is normally distributed around the mean
   (the PDF is gaussian).
 * Continuous uniform probability distribution between minimum value and maximum value and "0" probability beyond
   these.
 * Probability distribution of any random parameter between minimum value and maximum value with the highest
   probability at the average value of minimum plus maximum value. Linear change of probability between minimum,
   maximum and average value.
 * Means Impact is not known, but with >95% certainty the true value is below the declared value.
   So [1e-6,"kgCFC11e",0,"max"] means the ODP was not exactly measured, but it is guaranteed to be below
   1E-6 kg CO2e.  It is acceptable to treat a 'max' distribution a normal or lognormal distribution with variation
   0.1%.  This is conservative, because the 'max' value is usually much greater than the true impact.

classes.name: openepd.model.common.Measurement
documentation: A scientific value with units and uncertainty.

classes.name: openepd.model.common.IngredientEvidenceTypeEnum
documentation: Supported types of evidence for indirect ingredient.

classes.name: openepd.model.common.Ingredient
documentation:
An ingredient of a product.

The Ingredients list gives the core data references and quantities. This list is used to document supply-chain
transparency, such as the EPDs of major components (e.g. cement in concrete, or recycled steel
in hot-rolled sections).

Since the exact ingredients may be viewed as a proprietary information by Manufacturers, this schema also allows
to pass some data about ingredient about explicitly saying what it is. Further, this data can be used to
calculate the supply chain specificity of the product and uncertainty adjusted factor. For this option, use
gwp_fraction, citation and evidence_type.

classes.name: openepd.model.specs.range.concrete.CementGroutRangeV1
documentation:
Cement grout performance specification.

Cement grouting is a slurry that is placed as a flowable liquid. It is an effective material
for filling and strengthening granular soils, voids in rocks, foundation underpinnings, and
other underground voids. Also called structural grout, these materials typically impart
significant compressive strength to the system.

Range version.

classes.name: openepd.model.specs.range.concrete.ConcretePavingRangeV1
documentation:
Concrete paving.

Range version.

classes.name: openepd.model.specs.range.concrete.FlowableFillRangeV1
documentation:
Flowable fill performance specification.

Flowable fill is a slurry that is placed as a flowable liquid (high slump) and sets with no
compaction. It is often used in tight or restricted access areas where placing and compacting
fill is difficult. Applications include filling large voids such as abandoned underground storage
tanks, basements, tunnels, mines, and sewers. It can also be used as paving sub-base, bridge
abutment, and retaining wall backfill.

Also called Controlled Density Fill (CDF) or Controlled Low Strength Materials (CLSMs). These materials typically
have compressive strengths under 1200 psi.

Range version.

classes.name: openepd.model.specs.range.concrete.OilPatchRangeV1
documentation:
Petroleum Industry Cement Slurry.

Concretes for use in creation, maintenance, and decommissioning of petroleum extraction wells and similar
applications. Includes foamed cement; often called cement in the drilling industry. Differs from
flowable fill and grout in that it contains no sand or other aggregates.

Range version.

variables.name: openepd.api.base_sync_client.ErrorHandler
documentation:
ErrorHandler is a callable that takes a response and a boolean indicating whether 
exception should be raised in case of error.

classes.name: openepd.api.base_sync_client.HttpStreamReader

methods.name: openepd.api.base_sync_client.HttpStreamReader.__init__
documentation:
Initialize HttpStreamReader with given HttpResponse in streaming mode.

```
param http_response

HTTP response object
```

methods.name: openepd.api.base_sync_client.HttpStreamReader.readable
documentation: Return True if the stream can be read from.

methods.name: openepd.api.base_sync_client.HttpStreamReader.seekable
documentation: Return True if the stream supports random access.

methods.name: openepd.api.base_sync_client.HttpStreamReader.writable
documentation: Return True if the stream supports writing.

methods.name: openepd.api.base_sync_client.HttpStreamReader.read
documentation: Read and return up to size bytes, where size is an int.

methods.name: openepd.api.base_sync_client.HttpStreamReader.readinto
documentation: Read bytes into a pre-allocated, writable bytes-like object target_stream.

methods.name: openepd.api.base_sync_client.HttpStreamReader.raw
documentation: Return the underlying HTTP Response content.

methods.name: openepd.api.base_sync_client.HttpStreamReader.detach
documentation: Separate the underlying HTTP Response from the HttpStreamReader and return it.

methods.name: openepd.api.base_sync_client.HttpStreamReader.close
documentation:
Releases the connection back to the pool.

Once this method has been called the underlying `raw` object must not be accessed again.
*Note: Should not normally need to be called explicitly.*

methods.name: openepd.api.base_sync_client.HttpStreamReader.get_size
documentation: Return the size of the response in bytes.

methods.name: openepd.api.base_sync_client.HttpStreamReader.get_content_type
documentation: Return the content type of the response.

methods.name: openepd.api.base_sync_client.HttpStreamReader.get_status_code
documentation: Return the status code of the response.

methods.name: openepd.api.base_sync_client.HttpStreamReader.get_headers
documentation: Return the headers of the response.
A wrapper around a requests Response object that allows it to be used as a stream.

classes.name: openepd.api.base_sync_client.TokenAuth
documentation: Attach OpenEPD Token Authentication to the given Request object.

classes.name: openepd.api.base_sync_client.SyncHttpClient

methods.name: openepd.api.base_sync_client.SyncHttpClient.__init__
documentation:
Construct BaseApiClient.

```
param base_url

common part that all request URLs start with

param throttle_retry_timeout

how long to wait before retrying throttled request.
Either number of seconds or timedelta

param requests_per_sec

requests per second

param user_agent

user agent to pass along with a request,
if  then underlying library decides which one to pass

param timeout_sec

how long to wait for the server to send data before giving up,
as a seconds (just a single float), or a (connect timeout, read timeout) tuple.

param retry_count

count of retries to perform in case of connection error or timeout.
```

methods.name: openepd.api.base_sync_client.SyncHttpClient.base_url
documentation: Return base URL for all requests.

methods.name: openepd.api.base_sync_client.SyncHttpClient.base_url
documentation: Set base URL for all requests.

methods.name: openepd.api.base_sync_client.SyncHttpClient.default_headers
documentation: Default headers for requests. Implement if required.

methods.name: openepd.api.base_sync_client.SyncHttpClient.reset_session
documentation: Reset current session (if any). This will clear all cookies and other session data.

methods.name: openepd.api.base_sync_client.SyncHttpClient.read_bytes_from_url
documentation:
Perform query to the given endpoint and returns response body as bytes.

This method will load the ENTIRE CONTENT IN MEMORY. DO NOT USE it to download files. Consider using
`write_to_stream` instead.
The request will be performed in the context of API client, default error handling will be applied.

```
param url

url pointing the target endpoint

param method

optional HTTP method

param kwargs

any other arguments supported by _do_request. See .

return

response content as bytes
```

methods.name: openepd.api.base_sync_client.SyncHttpClient.read_url_write_to_stream
documentation:
Perform query to the given endpoint and writes response body to the given stream.

```
param url

url pointing the target endpoint

param target_stream

stream to write response body to

param method

optional HTTP method

param chunk_size

size of the chunk to read from the response

param kwargs

any other arguments supported by _do_request. See .

return

number of bytes written
```

methods.name: openepd.api.base_sync_client.SyncHttpClient.read_stream_from_url
documentation:
Perform query to the given endpoint and returns response body as a stream.

The request will be performed in the context of API client, default error handling will be applied.
NOTE: Consider using it as a context manager to handle stream close correctly.

```
param url

url pointing the target endpoint

param method

optional HTTP method

param kwargs

any other arguments supported by _do_request. See .

return

response content as a stream
```

methods.name: openepd.api.base_sync_client.SyncHttpClient.register_retry_handler
documentation:
Register retry handler for the given HTTP error code.

This allows to override default error handling for the given HTTP error code from the subclass.

methods.name: openepd.api.base_sync_client.SyncHttpClient.delete_retry_handler
documentation:
Delete retry handler for the given HTTP error code.

See _register_error_handler for more details.

methods.name: openepd.api.base_sync_client.SyncHttpClient.register_error_handler
documentation:
Register retry handler for the given HTTP error code.

This allows to override default error handling for the given HTTP error code from the subclass.

methods.name: openepd.api.base_sync_client.SyncHttpClient._delete_error_handler
documentation:
Delete retry handler for the given HTTP error code.

See _register_error_handler for more details.

methods.name: openepd.api.base_sync_client.SyncHttpClient.do_request
documentation:
Perform request to the given endpoint.

See https://requests.readthedocs.io/en/master/api/#requests.request for more details on kwargs.

methods.name: openepd.api.base_sync_client.SyncHttpClient._get_url_for_request
documentation:
Generate url for given input.

If absolute path is given it will be returned as is, otherwise the base url will be prepended.
\:param path_or_url: Either absolute url or base path.
\:return: absolute url

methods.name: openepd.api.base_sync_client.SyncHttpClient._on_before_do_request
documentation:
Perform any actions before request is sent.

This is a hook that will be called before `do_request`. Can be overridden to check / refresh access tokens.

HTTP client to communicate with OpenEPD servers via HTTP.

It works on top of requests library and provides some commonly needed features, such as throttling, retries, etc.

classes.name: openepd.api.base_sync_client.BaseApiMethodGroup

methods.name: openepd.api.base_sync_client.BaseApiMethodGroup.__init__
documentation:
Construct a method group.

```
param client

HTTP client to use for requests
```
Base class for API method groups.

Membrane roofing.

Built-up bituminous, modified bituminous, elastomeric, thermoplastic, fluid-applied, and hot-applied rubberized
asphalt membrane roofing.

Range version.

classes.name: openepd.model.specs.range.thermal_moisture_protection.InsulationRangeV1
documentation:
Thermal insulation materials for use in construction.

Range version.

classes.name: openepd.model.specs.range.thermal_moisture_protection.DampproofingAndWaterproofingRangeV1
documentation:
Dampproofing and waterproofing.

Dampproofing, and built-up bituminous, sheet, fluid-applied, cementitious, reactive, and bentonite waterproofing.

Range version.

classes.name: openepd.model.specs.range.thermal_moisture_protection.FlashingAndSheetMetalRangeV1
documentation:
Exposed sheet metal items, typically for drainage.

Range version.

classes.name: openepd.model.specs.range.thermal_moisture_protection.JointProtectionRangeV1
documentation:
Preformed joint seals and sealants, expansion control joint cover assemblies.

Range version.

classes.name: openepd.model.specs.range.thermal_moisture_protection.RoofCoverBoardsRangeV1
documentation:
Boards installed between the insulation and membrane layers on a roof system.

It provides additional durability, fire protection, thermal, and vapor performance to a roof system, especially
in low-slope, high foot traffic applications.

Range version.

classes.name: openepd.model.specs.range.thermal_moisture_protection.SteepSlopeRoofingRangeV1
documentation:
Roofing materials typically for slopes of 3:12 and greater.

Range version.

classes.name: openepd.model.specs.range.thermal_moisture_protection.WeatherBarriersRangeV1
documentation:
Vapor retarders and sheet or membrane air barriers.

Range version.

classes.name: openepd.model.specs.range.thermal_moisture_protection.ThermalMoistureProtectionRangeV1
documentation:
Thermal moisture protection.

Broad category of materials whose function is to provide moisture and thermal protection between spaces (e.g.,
between the exterior and interior of a building).

Range version.

Piping.

System of pipes used to provide water and fuel, remove wastewater, allow venting of gases, or supply

documentation:
Cast roof deck substrate systems.

Typically made of gyspum concrete or cementitious wood fiber, that provide structural support to
roofing materials.

documentation:
Wood joists.

Prefabricated I-shaped engineered wood structural members made primarily from one or more types of wood. Includes
products made with metallic webbing. Excludes products where the wood is merely decorative.

Range version.

classes.name: openepd.model.specs.range.fire_and_smoke_protection.IntumescentFireproofingRangeV1
documentation:
Fireproofing material applied to structural materials, which swells as a result of heat exposure.

As a result it increases in volume and decreasing in density.

Range version.

classes.name: openepd.model.specs.range.fire_and_smoke_protection.SprayFireproofingRangeV1
documentation:
Spray fireproofing.

A passive fire protection system that reduces the rate of temperature increase in concrete or steel during a fire.

Range version.

classes.name: openepd.model.specs.range.fire_and_smoke_protection.AppliedFireproofingRangeV1
documentation:
Fireproofing material applied to structural materials.

Materials include: cement aggregate, cementitious, magnesium-oxychloride, intumescent, magnesium cement, mineral
fiber, and mineral fiber fireproofing products.

Range version.

classes.name: openepd.model.specs.range.fire_and_smoke_protection.FirestoppingRangeV1
documentation:
Seals and protects openings and joints in fire rate assemblies.

Typically sealants, sprays, and caulks.

Range version.

classes.name: openepd.model.specs.range.fire_and_smoke_protection.FireAndSmokeProtectionRangeV1
documentation:
Fire and smoke protection.

General category of materials whose function is to provide protection of materials, spaces, and occupants from
fire and smoke damage.

Range version.

classes.name: openepd.model.specs.range.precast_concrete.UtilityUndergroundPrecastRangeV1

classes.name: openepd.model.specs.range.openings.PanelDoorsRangeV1
documentation:
Panel doors performance specification.

Range version.

classes.name: openepd.model.specs.range.openings.PressureResistantDoorsRangeV1
documentation:
Pressure-Resistant Doors.

Range version.

classes.name: openepd.model.specs.range.openings.SpecialFunctionDoorsRangeV1
documentation:
Special function doors.

Includes doors for e.g., cold storage, hangars, lightproof applications,
security, sound control, vaults, etc.

Range version.

classes.name: openepd.model.specs.range.openings.SlidingGlassDoorsRangeV1
documentation:
Sliding glass doors performance specification.

Range version.

classes.name: openepd.model.specs.range.openings.FenestrationAccessoriesRangeV1
documentation:
Fenestration accessories.

Gaskets, seals, fasteners, and other low-mass items which may be useful in calculating the impact of a
fenestration system.

Range version.

classes.name: openepd.model.specs.range.openings.FenestrationFramingRangeV1
documentation:
Fenestration Framing.

Lineal elements ("sticks") for use in fenestration, including frames, sashes, and mullions.

Range version.

classes.name: openepd.model.specs.range.openings.FenestrationHardwareRangeV1
documentation:
Locks, operation hardware, and other substantial items declared on a per-piece basis.

Range version.

classes.name: openepd.model.specs.range.openings.FlatGlassPanesRangeV1
documentation:
Monolithic, uncoated flat glass panes that are not substantially processed.

Range version.

classes.name: openepd.model.specs.range.openings.ProcessedNonInsulatingGlassPanesRangeV1
documentation:
Solid glass panes without internal gaps which have been heat-treated or otherwise substantially processed.

Includes:
1. Coatings including low-e and other coatings (see PCR)
2. laminating (fire-rated, glass clad polycarbonate, interlayers
3. Heat treated (heat strengthened, tempered, fire-rated)
4. Mechanically or chemically processed or fabricated
(edging, bending, etching, drilling, notching, cutting, polishing, etc)
5. combined products of processing in 1-5.

Range version.

classes.name: openepd.model.specs.range.openings.GlazedDoorsRangeV1
documentation:
Factory assembled door which is at least 50% glass by area.

Includes sliding patio doors and hinged doors.

Range version.

classes.name: openepd.model.specs.range.openings.UnitSkylightsRangeV1
documentation:
Unit skylights performance specification.

A factory assembled fenestration unit for installation on the roof of a structure to provide interior
building spaces with natural daylight, warmth, and ventilation; generally not operable
by hand (cf. roof window). Includes frame(s) and possibly operating hardware.

Range version.

classes.name: openepd.model.specs.range.openings.WindowsRangeV1
documentation:
Windows including glazing and frame material.

Range version.

classes.name: openepd.model.specs.range.openings.IntegratedDoorsOpeningAssembliesRangeV1
documentation:
Pre-installed unit that includes door, frame, and hardware.

Range version.

classes.name: openepd.model.specs.range.openings.MetalDoorAndFramesRangeV1
documentation:
Metal doors and frames.

Range version.

classes.name: openepd.model.specs.range.openings.SpecialtyDoorsAndFramesRangeV1
documentation:
Specialty doors and frames.

Includes e.g., access doors and panels, sliding glass doors, coiling doors, special function doors,
folding doors, etc.

Range version.

classes.name: openepd.model.specs.range.openings.WoodDoorsRangeV1
documentation:
Wood doors performance specification.

Range version.

classes.name: openepd.model.specs.range.openings.FenestrationPartsRangeV1
documentation:
Fenestration Parts.

Parts and assemblies for integration into building fenestration such as windows, curtain walls,
and storefronts.

Range version.

classes.name: openepd.model.specs.range.openings.GlassPanesRangeV1
documentation:
Flat glass panes.

Range version.

classes.name: openepd.model.specs.range.openings.NAFSFenestrationRangeV1
documentation:
Factory assembled fenestration units compliant to the North American Fenestration Standard.

Range version.

classes.name: openepd.model.specs.range.openings.InsulatingGlazingUnitsRangeV1
documentation:
Insulating glazing units performance specification.

Range version.

classes.name: openepd.model.specs.range.openings.CurtainWallsRangeV1
documentation:
Curtain Walls.

Exterior skin of building where walls are non-structural and are outboard of the floor slabs,
often as system of aluminum framing with vision glass and opaque panels of glass, metal, or other
materials.

Can be 'unitized' (prefabricated off-site) or 'stick' (fabricated on site).

Range version.

classes.name: openepd.model.specs.range.openings.DoorsAndFramesRangeV1
documentation:
Doors (the operable part) and frames (what holds the door proper).

Range version.

classes.name: openepd.model.specs.range.openings.EntrancesRangeV1
documentation:
Building entrances (distinct from the door proper).

Range version.

classes.name: openepd.model.specs.range.openings.GlazingRangeV1
documentation:
Glazing performance specification.

Broad category of glass-based products, accessories, and assemblies ranging from
glass panes and framing to curtain walls.

Range version.

classes.name: openepd.model.specs.range.openings.StorefrontsRangeV1
documentation:
Storefronts.

Fabricated building facades commonly used in retail applications, typically one or two stories
tall and using metal framing and glass.

Range version.

classes.name: openepd.model.specs.range.openings.TranslucentWallAndRoofAssembliesRangeV1
documentation:
Translucent wall and roof assemblies.

Includes structured polycarbonate panel and fiberglass sandwich panel assemblies.

Range version.

classes.name: openepd.model.specs.range.openings.WindowWallAssembliesRangeV1
documentation:
Window Wall Assemblies.

Exterior skin of building where walls are non-structural and sit between floor slabs, often as
system of aluminum framing with vision glass and opaque panels of glass, metal,
or other materials.

Range version.

classes.name: openepd.model.specs.range.openings.OpeningsRangeV1
documentation:
Openings performance specification.

General category that includes windows, storefronts, window walls, curtain walls,
doors, entrances, etc.

Range version.

classes.name: openepd.model.specs.range.mechanical.HvacVrfControlRangeV1
documentation:
Controller for adjusting airflow across the VRF system.

Range version.

classes.name: openepd.model.specs.range.mechanical.HvacVrfIndoorRangeV1
documentation:
Heating and cooling unit located on the inside of a building and supplies air to specific indoor zones.

Range version.

classes.name: openepd.model.specs.range.mechanical.HvacVrfOutdoorRangeV1
documentation:
Heating and cooling unit that is on the outside of a building and distributes air to the indoor units.

Range version.

classes.name: openepd.model.specs.range.mechanical.HvacAirDiffusersRangeV1
documentation:
Room-side terminals for air distribution. This is different from Terminal Heating & Cooling Units.

Range version.

classes.name: openepd.model.specs.range.mechanical.HvacAirFiltersRangeV1
documentation:
Device for filtering particles of dust, soot, etc., from the air passing through it.

Range version.

classes.name: openepd.model.specs.range.mechanical.HvacAHUsRangeV1
documentation:
Device which provides healthy, dust free air to buildings with a good energy efficiency.

Usually a large metal box containing a blower, heating or cooling elements, filter racks or chambers, sound
attenuators, and dampers.

Range version.

classes.name: openepd.model.specs.range.mechanical.HvacBoilersRangeV1
documentation:
Closed vessel for heating fluid.

Range version.

classes.name: openepd.model.specs.range.mechanical.HvacChillersRangeV1
documentation:
Machine that removes heat from a liquid coolant.

Uses a vapor-compression, adsorption refrigeration, or absorption refrigeration cycles. Incldues centrifugal,
water-cooled chillers, etc.

Range version.

classes.name: openepd.model.specs.range.mechanical.HvacFansRangeV1
documentation:
Apparatus with rotating blades that creates a current of air for cooling or ventilation.

Range version.

classes.name: openepd.model.specs.range.mechanical.HvacHeatPumpsRangeV1
documentation:
Device that transfers thermal energy between spaces, including ground and air source heat pumps.

Range version.

classes.name: openepd.model.specs.range.mechanical.HvacHeatExRangeV1
documentation:
HVAC heat exchangers.

Systems that with heat exchange cell which recovers and retains the heat that would otherwise be lost from the
extracted air.

Range version.

classes.name: openepd.model.specs.range.mechanical.HvacPumpsRangeV1
documentation:
Pumps.

Mechanical device using suction or pressure to raise or move liquids, compress gases, or force air into
inflatable objects such as tires.

Range version.

classes.name: openepd.model.specs.range.mechanical.HvacRTUsRangeV1
documentation:
An air handler designed for outdoor use, typically on roofs.

Range version.

classes.name: openepd.model.specs.range.mechanical.HvacVrfSystemsRangeV1
documentation:
Variable refrigerant flow (VRF).

Also known as variable refrigerant volume (VRV), is an HVAC technology that allows for varying degrees of cooling
in more specific areas.

Range version.

classes.name: openepd.model.specs.range.mechanical.HvacDuctsRangeV1
documentation:
Ducts for HVAC systems performance specification.

Range version.

classes.name: openepd.model.specs.range.mechanical.MechanicalRangeV1
documentation:
Mechanical performance specification.

Range version.

classes.name: openepd.model.specs.range.wood.WoodDeckingRangeV1
documentation:
Dimensional boards for exterior decking.

Range version.

classes.name: openepd.model.specs.range.wood.WoodFramingRangeV1
documentation:
Dimension lumber for light framing.

Includes solid and finger-jointed lumber. Standard shapes include 2x4, 2x6, and 2x8.

Range version.

classes.name: openepd.model.specs.range.wood.PrefabricatedWoodInsulatedPanelsRangeV1
documentation:
Structural insulated panels (SIPs).

Consist of wood sheet layer(s) combined with (typically foam) insulation layer(s).

Range version.

classes.name: openepd.model.specs.range.wood.PrefabricatedWoodTrussRangeV1
documentation:
Shop-fabricated wood truss.

Range version.

classes.name: openepd.model.specs.range.wood.CompositeLumberRangeV1
documentation:
Shop-fabricated structural Lumber.

Includes Laminated Strand Lumber (LSL), Parallel Strand Lumber (PSL), and Laminated Veneer Lumber (LVL).

Range version.

classes.name: openepd.model.specs.range.wood.DimensionLumberRangeV1
documentation:
Dimension lumber for framing, decking, and other purposes.

Range version.

classes.name: openepd.model.specs.range.wood.HeavyTimberRangeV1
documentation:
Large format, unfinished natural timber.

Range version.

classes.name: openepd.model.specs.range.wood.MassTimberRangeV1
documentation:
Engineered heavy timber products.

Includes glue laminated (glulam), cross-laminated timber (CLT), dowel laminated timber (DLT), and nail laminated
timber (NLT).

Range version.

classes.name: openepd.model.specs.range.wood.NonStructuralWoodRangeV1
documentation:
Non-structural interior and exterior wood products for trim, cabinets, countertops, etc.

Range version.

classes.name: openepd.model.specs.range.wood.PrefabricatedWoodRangeV1
documentation:
Prefabricated wood structural members made primarily from one or more types of wood.

Includes products made with metallic connectors, insulation, etc. Excludes products where the wood is merely
decorative.

Range version.

classes.name: openepd.model.specs.range.wood.SheathingPanelsRangeV1
documentation:
Wood sheets used for structural sheathing, including plywood and Oriented Strand Board.

Range version.

classes.name: openepd.model.specs.range.wood.UnfinishedWoodRangeV1
documentation:
Raw logs and other unfinished wood products.

Range version.

classes.name: openepd.model.specs.range.wood.WoodRangeV1
documentation:
Structural Wood Products used in construction.

Range version.

(i.e., superplasticizers).

Range version.

classes.name: openepd.model.specs.singular.concrete.CementGroutV1
documentation:
Cement grout performance specification.

Cement grouting is a slurry that is placed as a flowable liquid. It is an effective material
for filling and strengthening granular soils, voids in rocks, foundation underpinnings, and
other underground voids. Also called structural grout, these materials typically impart
significant compressive strength to the system.

classes.name: openepd.model.specs.singular.concrete.ConcretePavingV1
documentation: Concrete paving.

classes.name: openepd.model.specs.singular.concrete.FlowableFillV1
documentation:
Flowable fill performance specification.

Flowable fill is a slurry that is placed as a flowable liquid (high slump) and sets with no
compaction. It is often used in tight or restricted access areas where placing and compacting
fill is difficult. Applications include filling large voids such as abandoned underground storage
tanks, basements, tunnels, mines, and sewers. It can also be used as paving sub-base, bridge
abutment, and retaining wall backfill.

Also called Controlled Density Fill (CDF) or Controlled Low Strength Materials (CLSMs). These materials typically
have compressive strengths under 1200 psi.

classes.name: openepd.model.specs.singular.concrete.OilPatchV1
documentation:
Petroleum Industry Cement Slurry.

Concretes for use in creation, maintenance, and decommissioning of petroleum extraction wells and similar
applications. Includes foamed cement; often called cement in the drilling industry. Differs from
flowable fill and grout in that it contains no sand or other aggregates.

classes.name: openepd.model.specs.singular.concrete.ReadyMixV1
documentation: Concretes to be mixed and then poured on-site.

classes.name: openepd.model.specs.singular.concrete.ShotcreteV1
documentation: Concretes sprayed on a target.

classes.name: openepd.model.specs.singular.concrete.ConcreteV1
documentation:
Concrete.

A composite material composed of fine and coarse aggregate bonded together with a fluid cement (cement paste) that
hardens over time.

functions.name: openepd.model.specs.singular.deprecated.get_safely
documentation:
Get a value from a mixed object via dotted path.

Mixed object can be a combination of dicts and pydatnic Models on any hierarchy.
\:param d: source dict/object to search in
\:param path: dotted path in object like specs.Concrete.strength_28d
\:return: tuple (WasFound, Value). First element tells if value was found, second - the value itself.

functions.name: openepd.model.specs.singular.deprecated.set_safely
documentation:
Safely set an element in a dict.

Warning: this is asymmetric compared to get_safely, since it sets value in a dict, not in pydantic model object.

```
param d

source dict/object to set value in

param path

dotted path in object like specs.Concrete.strength_28d

param value

value to set

return

None
```

Flat hot-rolled steel, typically thicker than 'sheet', made by compressing multiple steel

Lightweight steel truss, typically made of parallel chords and a triangulated web system,

Used as feedstock to steel construction products, but also includes rounds, angles, and light structural shapes.

Typically, coil steel is cold-formed into light gauge products.

classes.name: openepd.model.specs.singular.steel.ColdFormedSteelV1

All types of cold formed steel products. These products are made from hot-rolled steel coils and
sheets and are cold formed into products such as studs, decking, panels, and other accessories.

classes.name: openepd.model.specs.singular.steel.StructuralSteelV1

Hot rolled steel shapes, Hollow Sections, pipes, and similar hot-worked structural steels.

classes.name: openepd.model.specs.singular.steel.PrefabricatedSteelAssembliesV1

classes.name: openepd.model.specs.singular.steel.PostTensioningSteelV1

classes.name: openepd.model.specs.singular.steel.RebarSteelV1

Dampproofing, and built-up bituminous, sheet, fluid-applied, cementitious, reactive, and bentonite waterproofing.

Boards installed between the insulation and membrane layers on a roof system.

It provides additional durability, fire protection, thermal, and vapor performance to a roof system, especially
in low-slope, high foot traffic applications.

classes.name: openepd.model.specs.singular.thermal_moisture_protection.SteepSlopeRoofingV1
documentation: Roofing materials typically for slopes of 3:12 and greater.

classes.name: openepd.model.specs.singular.thermal_moisture_protection.WeatherBarriersV1
documentation: Vapor retarders and sheet or membrane air barriers.

classes.name: openepd.model.specs.singular.thermal_moisture_protection.ThermalMoistureProtectionV1
documentation:
Thermal moisture protection.

Broad category of materials whose function is to provide moisture and thermal protection between spaces (e.g.,
between the exterior and interior of a building).

classes.name: openepd.model.specs.singular.plumbing.PlumbingV1

methods.name: openepd.model.specs.singular.Specs._ensure_backward_compatibiltiy
documentation:
Restore the functionality for backward-compatible specs.

Originally, we used to have 'concrete' and 'steel' specs non-hierarchical and manually maintained. Since we
introduced hierarchical specs, there is a need to retain the key mapping to the old structure.

```
return

modified values
```
Material specific specs.

Prefabricated I-shaped engineered wood structural members made primarily from one or more types of wood. Includes
products made with metallic webbing. Excludes products where the wood is merely decorative.

documentation:
Cast roof deck substrate systems.

Typically made of gyspum concrete or cementitious wood fiber, that provide structural support to
roofing materials.

classes.name: openepd.model.specs.singular.fire_and_smoke_protection.IntumescentFireproofingV1
documentation:
Fireproofing material applied to structural materials, which swells as a result of heat exposure.

As a result it increases in volume and decreasing in density.

classes.name: openepd.model.specs.singular.fire_and_smoke_protection.SprayFireproofingV1
documentation:
Spray fireproofing.

A passive fire protection system that reduces the rate of temperature increase in concrete or steel during a fire.

classes.name: openepd.model.specs.singular.fire_and_smoke_protection.AppliedFireproofingV1
documentation:
Fireproofing material applied to structural materials.

Materials include: cement aggregate, cementitious, magnesium-oxychloride, intumescent, magnesium cement, mineral
fiber, and mineral fiber fireproofing products.

classes.name: openepd.model.specs.singular.fire_and_smoke_protection.FirestoppingV1
documentation:
Seals and protects openings and joints in fire rate assemblies.

Typically sealants, sprays, and caulks.

classes.name: openepd.model.specs.singular.fire_and_smoke_protection.FireAndSmokeProtectionV1
documentation:
Fire and smoke protection.

General category of materials whose function is to provide protection of materials, spaces, and occupants from
fire and smoke damage.

classes.name: openepd.model.specs.singular.openings.GlazingIntendedApplication
documentation: Glass intended application mixin.

classes.name: openepd.model.specs.singular.openings.GlazingOptionsMixin
documentation: Common glazing options.

classes.name: openepd.model.specs.singular.openings.PanelDoorsV1
documentation: Panel doors performance specification.

classes.name: openepd.model.specs.singular.openings.PressureResistantDoorsV1
documentation: Pressure-Resistant Doors.

classes.name: openepd.model.specs.singular.openings.SpecialFunctionDoorsV1
documentation:
Special function doors.

Includes doors for e.g., cold storage, hangars, lightproof applications,
security, sound control, vaults, etc.

classes.name: openepd.model.specs.singular.openings.SlidingGlassDoorsV1
documentation: Sliding glass doors performance specification.

classes.name: openepd.model.specs.singular.openings.FenestrationAccessoriesV1
documentation:
Fenestration accessories.

Gaskets, seals, fasteners, and other low-mass items which may be useful in calculating the impact of a
fenestration system.

classes.name: openepd.model.specs.singular.openings.FenestrationFramingV1
documentation:
Fenestration Framing.

Lineal elements ("sticks") for use in fenestration, including frames, sashes, and mullions.

classes.name: openepd.model.specs.singular.openings.FenestrationHardwareV1
documentation: Locks, operation hardware, and other substantial items declared on a per-piece basis.

classes.name: openepd.model.specs.singular.openings.FlatGlassPanesV1
documentation: Monolithic, uncoated flat glass panes that are not substantially processed.

classes.name: openepd.model.specs.singular.openings.ProcessedNonInsulatingGlassPanesV1
documentation:
Solid glass panes without internal gaps which have been heat-treated or otherwise substantially processed.

Includes:
1. Coatings including low-e and other coatings (see PCR)
2. laminating (fire-rated, glass clad polycarbonate, interlayers
3. Heat treated (heat strengthened, tempered, fire-rated)
4. Mechanically or chemically processed or fabricated
(edging, bending, etching, drilling, notching, cutting, polishing, etc)
5. combined products of processing in 1-5.

classes.name: openepd.model.specs.singular.openings.GlazedDoorsV1
documentation:
Factory assembled door which is at least 50% glass by area.

Includes sliding patio doors and hinged doors.

classes.name: openepd.model.specs.singular.openings.UnitSkylightsV1
documentation:
Unit skylights performance specification.

A factory assembled fenestration unit for installation on the roof of a structure to provide interior
building spaces with natural daylight, warmth, and ventilation; generally not operable
by hand (cf. roof window). Includes frame(s) and possibly operating hardware.

classes.name: openepd.model.specs.singular.openings.WindowsV1
documentation: Windows including glazing and frame material.

classes.name: openepd.model.specs.singular.openings.IntegratedDoorsOpeningAssembliesV1
documentation: Pre-installed unit that includes door, frame, and hardware.

classes.name: openepd.model.specs.singular.openings.MetalDoorAndFramesV1
documentation: Metal doors and frames.

classes.name: openepd.model.specs.singular.openings.SpecialtyDoorsAndFramesV1
documentation:
Specialty doors and frames.

Includes e.g., access doors and panels, sliding glass doors, coiling doors, special function doors,
folding doors, etc.

classes.name: openepd.model.specs.singular.openings.WoodDoorsV1
documentation: Wood doors performance specification.

classes.name: openepd.model.specs.singular.openings.FenestrationPartsV1
documentation:
Fenestration Parts.

Parts and assemblies for integration into building fenestration such as windows, curtain walls,
and storefronts.

classes.name: openepd.model.specs.singular.openings.GlassPanesV1
documentation: Flat glass panes.

classes.name: openepd.model.specs.singular.openings.NAFSPerformanceClass
documentation: NAFS Performance class.

classes.name: openepd.model.specs.singular.openings.NAFSFenestrationV1
documentation: Factory assembled fenestration units compliant to the North American Fenestration Standard.

classes.name: openepd.model.specs.singular.openings.InsulatingGlazingUnitsV1
documentation: Insulating glazing units performance specification.

classes.name: openepd.model.specs.singular.openings.CurtainWallsV1
documentation:
Curtain Walls.

Exterior skin of building where walls are non-structural and are outboard of the floor slabs,
often as system of aluminum framing with vision glass and opaque panels of glass, metal, or other
materials.

Can be 'unitized' (prefabricated off-site) or 'stick' (fabricated on site).

classes.name: openepd.model.specs.singular.openings.DoorsAndFramesV1
documentation: Doors (the operable part) and frames (what holds the door proper).

classes.name: openepd.model.specs.singular.openings.EntrancesV1
documentation: Building entrances (distinct from the door proper).

classes.name: openepd.model.specs.singular.openings.GlazingV1
documentation:
Glazing performance specification.

Broad category of glass-based products, accessories, and assemblies ranging from
glass panes and framing to curtain walls.

classes.name: openepd.model.specs.singular.openings.StorefrontsV1
documentation:
Storefronts.

Fabricated building facades commonly used in retail applications, typically one or two stories
tall and using metal framing and glass.

classes.name: openepd.model.specs.singular.openings.TranslucentWallAndRoofAssembliesV1
documentation:
Translucent wall and roof assemblies.

Includes structured polycarbonate panel and fiberglass sandwich panel assemblies.

classes.name: openepd.model.specs.singular.openings.WindowWallAssembliesV1
documentation:
Window Wall Assemblies.

Exterior skin of building where walls are non-structural and sit between floor slabs, often as
system of aluminum framing with vision glass and opaque panels of glass, metal,
or other materials.

classes.name: openepd.model.specs.singular.openings.OpeningsV1
documentation:
Openings performance specification.

General category that includes windows, storefronts, window walls, curtain walls,
doors, entrances, etc.

classes.name: openepd.model.specs.singular.precast_concrete.StructuralPrecastV1

classes.name: openepd.model.specs.singular.precast_concrete.UtilityUndergroundPrecastV1

documentation: Precast concrete used for civil engineering applications including bridges, highways, and railroads.

documentation: General category for precast concrete components.

classes.name: openepd.model.specs.singular.mechanical.HvacVrfControlV1
documentation: Controller for adjusting airflow across the VRF system.

classes.name: openepd.model.specs.singular.mechanical.HvacVrfIndoorV1
documentation: Heating and cooling unit located on the inside of a building and supplies air to specific indoor zones.

classes.name: openepd.model.specs.singular.mechanical.HvacVrfOutdoorV1
documentation: Heating and cooling unit that is on the outside of a building and distributes air to the indoor units.

classes.name: openepd.model.specs.singular.mechanical.HvacAirDiffusersV1
documentation: Room-side terminals for air distribution. This is different from Terminal Heating & Cooling Units.

classes.name: openepd.model.specs.singular.mechanical.HvacAirFiltersV1
documentation: Device for filtering particles of dust, soot, etc., from the air passing through it.

classes.name: openepd.model.specs.singular.mechanical.HvacAHUsV1
documentation:
Device which provides healthy, dust free air to buildings with a good energy efficiency.

Usually a large metal box containing a blower, heating or cooling elements, filter racks or chambers, sound
attenuators, and dampers.

classes.name: openepd.model.specs.singular.mechanical.HvacBoilersV1
documentation: Closed vessel for heating fluid.

classes.name: openepd.model.specs.singular.mechanical.HvacChillersV1
documentation:
Machine that removes heat from a liquid coolant.

Uses a vapor-compression, adsorption refrigeration, or absorption refrigeration cycles. Incldues centrifugal,
water-cooled chillers, etc.

classes.name: openepd.model.specs.singular.mechanical.HvacFansV1
documentation: Apparatus with rotating blades that creates a current of air for cooling or ventilation.

classes.name: openepd.model.specs.singular.mechanical.HvacHeatPumpsV1
documentation: Device that transfers thermal energy between spaces, including ground and air source heat pumps.

classes.name: openepd.model.specs.singular.mechanical.HvacHeatExV1
documentation:
HVAC heat exchangers.

Systems that with heat exchange cell which recovers and retains the heat that would otherwise be lost from the
extracted air.

classes.name: openepd.model.specs.singular.mechanical.HvacPumpsV1
documentation:
Pumps.

Mechanical device using suction or pressure to raise or move liquids, compress gases, or force air into
inflatable objects such as tires.

classes.name: openepd.model.specs.singular.mechanical.HvacRTUsV1
documentation: An air handler designed for outdoor use, typically on roofs.

classes.name: openepd.model.specs.singular.mechanical.HvacVrfSystemsV1
documentation:
Variable refrigerant flow (VRF).

Also known as variable refrigerant volume (VRV), is an HVAC technology that allows for varying degrees of cooling
in more specific areas.

classes.name: openepd.model.specs.singular.mechanical.HvacDuctsV1
documentation: Ducts for HVAC systems performance specification.

classes.name: openepd.model.specs.singular.mechanical.MechanicalV1
documentation: Mechanical performance specification.

Machinery and tools designed to move materials within a facility, both manually and automatically.

This includes various types of conveyors such as belt, roller, and overhead conveyors.

classes.name: openepd.model.specs.singular.material_handling.MaterialHandlingV1
documentation:
Material handling.

Equipment and supplies for moving, storing, administering, and protecting materials during the handling process.

Escalators category performance specification.

A moving staircase consisting of a circulating belt of steps driven by a motor,
which conveys people between the floors of a building.

classes.name: openepd.model.specs.singular.conveying_equipment.ElevatorsV1
documentation: Car that moves in a vertical shaft to carry passengers or freight between the levels of a multistory building.

classes.name: openepd.model.specs.singular.conveying_equipment.ConveyingEquipmentV1
documentation: Conveying Equipment.

classes.name: openepd.model.specs.singular.wood.WoodDeckingV1
documentation: Dimensional boards for exterior decking.

classes.name: openepd.model.specs.singular.wood.WoodFramingV1
documentation:
Dimension lumber for light framing.

Includes solid and finger-jointed lumber. Standard shapes include 2x4, 2x6, and 2x8.

classes.name: openepd.model.specs.singular.wood.PrefabricatedWoodInsulatedPanelsV1
documentation:
Structural insulated panels (SIPs).

Consist of wood sheet layer(s) combined with (typically foam) insulation layer(s).

classes.name: openepd.model.specs.singular.wood.PrefabricatedWoodTrussV1
documentation: Shop-fabricated wood truss.

classes.name: openepd.model.specs.singular.wood.CompositeLumberV1
documentation:
Shop-fabricated structural Lumber.

Includes Laminated Strand Lumber (LSL), Parallel Strand Lumber (PSL), and Laminated Veneer Lumber (LVL).

classes.name: openepd.model.specs.singular.wood.DimensionLumberV1
documentation: Dimension lumber for framing, decking, and other purposes.

classes.name: openepd.model.specs.singular.wood.HeavyTimberV1
documentation: Large format, unfinished natural timber.

classes.name: openepd.model.specs.singular.wood.MassTimberV1
documentation:
Engineered heavy timber products.

Includes glue laminated (glulam), cross-laminated timber (CLT), dowel laminated timber (DLT), and nail laminated
timber (NLT).

classes.name: openepd.model.specs.singular.wood.NonStructuralWoodV1
documentation: Non-structural interior and exterior wood products for trim, cabinets, countertops, etc.

classes.name: openepd.model.specs.singular.wood.PrefabricatedWoodV1
documentation:
Prefabricated wood structural members made primarily from one or more types of wood.

Includes products made with metallic connectors, insulation, etc. Excludes products where the wood is merely
decorative.

classes.name: openepd.model.specs.singular.wood.SheathingPanelsV1
documentation: Wood sheets used for structural sheathing, including plywood and Oriented Strand Board.

classes.name: openepd.model.specs.singular.wood.UnfinishedWoodV1
documentation: Raw logs and other unfinished wood products.

classes.name: openepd.model.specs.singular.wood.WoodV1
documentation: Structural Wood Products used in construction.

classes.name: openepd.model.specs.singular.finishes.AccessFlooringV1
documentation:
Elevated floor system built on top of concrete slab surface.

It thereby creates a hidden void between the two floors that is used for the passage of mechanical and electrical
services. The system consists of panels, stringers, and pedestals.

Includes vinyl, rubber, linoleum, composition cork, etc. in modular square or rectangle shapes.

classes.name: openepd.model.specs.singular.finishes.WallBaseV1

Used for covering and enhancing surfaces such as floors, walls, and countertops.

Paintings and coatings by volume.

A cementitious product typically composed of Portland cement and hydrated lime.

Masonry cement is combined with sand to make mortar.

documentation: Cementitious materials that are not effective binders when used on their own.

classes.name: openepd.model.specs.singular.manufacturing_inputs.AccessFlooringPedestalsV1
documentation:
Part of an access floor system.

Pedestals are laid out on top of a floor slab and support access floor panels, creating a void space between the
floor slab and finish floor.

classes.name: openepd.model.specs.singular.manufacturing_inputs.CementitiousMaterialsV1

classes.name: openepd.model.specs.singular.manufacturing_inputs.ConcreteAdmixturesV1
documentation:
Concrete admixtures.

Chemical additives that are added to fresh concrete immediately before or during mixing. Admixtures have distinct
functions and are categorized as: air-entraining, water-reducing, retarding, accelerating, and plasticizers
(i.e., superplasticizers).

classes.name: openepd.model.specs.singular.manufacturing_inputs.TextilesV1

classes.name: openepd.model.specs.singular.manufacturing_inputs.ManufacturingInputsV1
documentation:
Manufacturing inputs.

Broad category for collecting materials primarily used as manufacturing inputs, rather than directly used in
a construction.

classes.name: openepd.model.industry_epd.IndustryEpdV0

Full Industry EPD object.

This is considered the most complete valid openEPD object for IndustryEpd. In addition to it, several related
models are defined, either with fewer fields (to be used in APIs for list requests) or with more relaxed structure
to support related entities matching.

classes.name: openepd.model.industry_epd.IndustryEpdWithDepsV0
documentation:
Expanded version of the IndustryEpd.

Contains related entities - orgs - with full fields, to support object matching in implementations.

For now the implementation matches the above Industry Epd entity, but they will diverge as normal GE would have
some required fields in Org (like web_domain), and WithDeps would not.

A list of available LCA methods.

classes.name: openepd.model.lcia.Impacts

methods.name: openepd.model.lcia.Impacts.set_unknown_lcia
documentation: Set the impact set as an unknown LCIA method.

methods.name: openepd.model.lcia.Impacts.set_impact_set
documentation:
Set the impact set for the given LCIA method.

If the LCIA method is None, set it as an unknown LCIA method.

methods.name: openepd.model.lcia.Impacts.replace_lcia_method
documentation:
Replace the LCIA method.

If the there is no impact set for the given LCIA method, do nothing.

methods.name: openepd.model.lcia.Impacts.get_impact_set
documentation: Return the impact set for the given LCIA method.

methods.name: openepd.model.lcia.Impacts.available_methods
documentation: Return a list of available LCIA methods.

methods.name: openepd.model.lcia.Impacts.as_dict
documentation: Return the impacts as a dictionary.

classes.name: openepd.model.specs.enums.HvacDuctType
documentation:
Hvac duct type.

- Flexible: Duckwork that can be bent or adjusted easily. Suitable for tricky, tight spaces where rigid
            ducts cannot be used.
- Rigid: Stiff ductwork, typically in fixed shapes. Commonly used for main duct runs due to its durability
         and airflow efficiency.
- Semi-rigid: Less flexible than flexible ducts but not fixed. Balances flexibility and sturdiness,
              useful for certain architectural constraints. Used mostly in residential applications.
- Ductboard: Insulated panel-type ductwork made from fiberglass or other materials. It provides thermal insulation
             and is used where space is not a constraint.

classes.name: openepd.model.specs.enums.HvacDuctMaterial
documentation:
Hvac duct material.

- Galvanized Steel: coated with a layer of zinc to prevent rust. Very durable and common for both residential
                    and commercial HVAC systems.
- Aluminum: lightweight and resistant to corrosion. Uncommon but preferred for special applications due
            to its ease of handling and installation.
- PVC: Plastic ducts suitable for specific environments. Uncommon but used where moisture or chemical resistance
       is needed. Not for high temperatures.
- Fiberglass: Insulated ducts that also absorb sound. Reduces noise and heat loss. Uncommon but suitable
              for sensitive environments.
- Fabric: Ducts made from specialized fabric materials. Used for distributing air uniformly, and in places
          requiring diffused airflow.

classes.name: openepd.model.specs.enums.HvacDuctShape
documentation:
Hvac duct shape.

- Round: Cylindrical ducts providing efficient airflow. Minimizes friction losses, making it effective for long runs.
- Rectangular: Square or rectangular ducts, versatile installation. Common in commercial settings, easier
               to install in flat spaces.
- Oval: Combines attributes of round and rectangular shapes. Provides the benefits of round ducts
        where space is limited.

classes.name: openepd.model.specs.enums.ThermalSeparation
documentation:
Thermal separation.

 - Aluminium: Aluminium System members with less than 1.6 mm (0.062 in) or no separation between metal or system
   members to provide a resistance heat transfer path from exterior to interior.
 - Steel: Steel System members with less than 1.6 mm (0.062 in) or no separation between metal or system members to
   provide a resistance heat transfer path from exterior to interior.
 - Thermally Improved Metal: Metal System members with a separation ≥ 1.60 mm (0.062 in) provided by either a
   thermal break or open air space that may be interrupted by short thermal bridges between the interior and
   exterior surfaces.
 - Thermally Broken Metal: Aluminium System members with a minimum of 5.30 mm (0.210 in) separation provided by
   a thermal break.
 - Nonmetal: Nonmetal material where the path from inside to outside inherently has low thermal conductivity,
   such as wood, vinyl, and fiberglass. Aluminium-clad or split windows fall in this category.

classes.name: openepd.model.specs.enums.AciExposureClass

methods.name: openepd.model.specs.enums.AciExposureClass.get_groupings
documentation: Return logical groupings of the values.

American Concrete Institute concrete exposure classes.

  * `aci.F0` - Concrete not subjected to freezing-and-thawing cycles
  * `aci.F1` - Concrete experiences freezing-and-thawing cycles with limited exposure to water
  * `aci.F2` - Concrete exposed to freezing-and-thawing cycles with frequent exposure to water
  * `aci.F3` - Concrete exposed to freezing-and-thawing cycles with continual exposure to water
                and exposure to deicing chemicals
  * `aci.S0` - Exposed to <150 ppm of SO4 in water and <0.1% SO4 in soil
  * `aci.S1` - Exposed to <1500 ppm of SO4 in water and <0.2% SO4 in soil
  * `aci.S2` - Exposed to <10000 ppm of SO4 in water and <2% SO4 in soil
  * `aci.S3` - Exposed to >10000 ppm of SO4 in water or >2% SO4 in soil

  * `aci.C0` - Concrete dry or protected from moisture.
  * `aci.C1` - Concrete in contact with moisture, but the external source of chloride does not reach it.
  * `aci.C2` - Concrete subjected to moisture and an external source of chlorides such as deicing chemicals,
                salt, brackish water, seawater, or spray from these sources.
  * `aci.W0` - Concrete dry in service
  * `aci.W1` - Concrete in contact with water, no requirement for low permeability
  * `aci.W2` - Concrete in contact with water where low permeability is required

classes.name: openepd.model.specs.enums.CsaExposureClass

methods.name: openepd.model.specs.enums.CsaExposureClass.get_groupings
documentation: Return logical groupings of the values.

Canadian Standard Association concrete exposure classes.

  * `csa.C-XL` - Structurally reinforced concrete exposed to chlorides or other severe environment with or without
                 freezing and thawing conditions, with higher durability performance expectations than the C-1, A-1
                 or S-1 classes.

  * `csa.C-1` - Structurally reinforced concrete exposed to chlorides with or without freezing and thawing conditions.
                Examples: bridge decks, parking decks and ramps, portions of marine structures located within the tidal
                and splash zones, concrete exposed to seawater spray, and salt water pools.
  * `csa.C-2` - Non-structurally reinforced (i.e. plain) concrete exposed to chlorides and freezing and thawing.
                Examples: garage floors, porches, steps, pavements, sidewalks curbs and gutters.
  * `csa.C-3` - Continuously submerged concrete exposed to chlorides but not to freezing and thawing.
                Example: underwater portions of marine structures.
  * `csa.C-4` - Non-structurally reinforced concrete exposed to chlorides but not to freezing and thawing.
                Examples: underground parking slabs on grade.

  * `csa.F-1` - Concrete exposed to freezing and thawing in a saturated condition but not to chlorides.
                Examples: pool decks, patios, tennis courts, freshwater pools and fresh water control structures.
  * `csa.F-2` - Concrete in an unsaturated condition exposed to freezing and thawing but not to chlorides.
                Examples: exterior walls and columns.

  * `csa.N` - Concrete not exposed to chlorides nor to freezing and thawing.
              Examples: footings and interior slabs, walls and columns.

  * `csa.A-1` - Structurally reinforced concrete exposed to severe manure and/or silage gases, with or without
                freeze-thaw exposure. Concrete exposed to the vapour above municipal sewage or industrial effluent,
                where hydrogen sulphide gas may be generated.
                Examples: reinforced beams, slabs, and columns over manure pits and silos, canals, and pig slats;
                and access holes, enclosed chambers and pipes that are partially filled with effluents.
  * `csa.A-2` - Structurally reinforced concrete exposed to moderate to severe manure and/or silage gases and liquids,
                with or without freeze-thaw exposure.
                Examples: reinforced walls in exterior manure tanks, silos and feed bunkers, and exterior slabs.
  * `csa.A-3` - Structurally reinforced concrete exposed to moderate to severe manure and/or silage gases and liquids,
                with or without freeze-thaw exposure in a continuously submerged condition. Concrete continuously
                submerged in municipal or industrial effluents.
                Examples: interior gutter walls, beams, slabs and columns; sewage pipes that are continuously full
                (e.g. force mains); and submerged portions of sewage treatment structures.
  * `csa.A-4` - Non-structurally reinforced concrete exposed to moderate manure and/or silage gases and liquids, without
                freeze-thaw exposure.
                Examples: interior slabs on grade.

  * `csa.S-1` - Concrete subjected to very severe sulphate exposures.
                Exposed to >10000 ppm of SO4 in water or >2% SO4 in soil
  * `csa.S-2` - Concrete subjected to severe sulphate exposure.
                Exposed to <10000 ppm of SO4 in water and <2% SO4 in soil
  * `csa.S-3` - Concrete subjected to moderate sulphate exposure.
                Exposed to <1500 ppm of SO4 in water and <0.2% SO4 in soil

classes.name: openepd.model.specs.enums.EnExposureClass

methods.name: openepd.model.specs.enums.EnExposureClass.get_groupings
documentation: Return logical groupings of the values.

EN 206 Class (Europe).

European Standard concrete exposure classes.

  * `en206.X0` - No risk of corrosion or attack.

  Corrosion induced by carbonation.

  * `en206.XC1` - Dry or permanently wet.
  * `en206.XC2` - Wet, rarely dry.
  * `en206.XC3` - Moderate humidity.
  * `en206.XC4` - Cyclic wet and dry.

  Corrosion induced by chlorides from sea water.

  * `en206.XS1` - Exposed to airborne salt but not in direct contact with sea water.
  * `en206.XS2` - Permanently submerged.
  * `en206.XS3` - Tidal, splash and spray zones.

  Corrosion induced by chlorides other than from sea water.

  * `en206.XD1` - Moderate humidity.
  * `en206.XD2` - Wet, rarely dry.
  * `en206.XD3` - Cyclic wet and dry.

  Freeze/thaw attack with or without de-icing agents.

  * `en206.XF1` - Moderate water saturation, without deicing agent.
  * `en206.XF2` - Moderate water saturation, with deicing agent.
  * `en206.XF3` - High water saturation, without de-icing agent.
  * `en206.XF4` - High water saturation, with de-icing agent or sea water.

  Chemical attack.

  * `en206.XA1` - Slightly aggressive chemical environment.
  * `en206.XA2` - Moderately aggressive chemical environment.
  * `en206.XA3` - Highly aggressive chemical environment.

classes.name: openepd.model.specs.enums.AggregateWeightClassification
documentation:
Aggregate weight classification enum.

- Lightweight: Also called "low-density aggregate". Aggregate with bulk density less than 1120 kg/m3 such as:
  pumice, scoria, volcanic cinders, tuff, and diatomite; expanded or sintered clay, shale, slate, diatomaceous
  shale, perlite, vermiculite, or slag; and end products of coal or coke combustion.
- Normal-weight: Aggregate that is between the densities of lightweight and heavy-weight
- Heavy-weight: Also called "high-density aggregate". Aggregate with bulk density greater than 1920 kg/m3 such as:
  barite, magnetite, limonite, ilmenite, iron, or steel.

classes.name: openepd.model.specs.enums.AggregateGradation
documentation:
Aggregate gradation enum.

- Dense: Grading in which the void percentage between aggregate particles is minimized.
- Gap: Grading in which one or more intermediate size fractions are omitted.
- Open: Grading with a large amount of air voids, which allows water drainage.

functions.name: openepd.model.validation.enum.exclusive_groups_validator_factory
documentation:
Create an exclusive groups validator.

If we have a certain enum TheEnum, and a field of list[TheEnum], where list can contain only one value of each of
the groups, this validator should be used. For example, ACI exposure classes for concrete specify various
parameters such as water resistance, chemical resistance, etc., and the list of classes can have 0 or 1 from each
group.

\:param enum_type:Enum type which supports groupings.
\:return:value, or raises ValueError if not allowed combination is given.

functions.name: openepd.patch_pydantic.patch_pydantic_metaclass
documentation:
Patch pydantic's ModelMetaclass to restore class attribute lookup for fields.

In pydantic, while the model fields are defined in the model as class-level attributes, in runtime they disappear
due to ModelMetaclass logic. ModelMetaclass takes the defined attributes, removes them from class dict and puts
into a special __fields__ attribute to avoid naming conflict.

We would like to be able to access the attributes via dot notation in the runtimes, since it makes refactoring
easier.

This class exposes the original fields when accessed via class name. For example, one can call `Pcr.name` and get
`ModelField`, in addition to calling `pcr.__fields__` on an instance.

functions.name: openepd.patch_pydantic.patch_pydantic_metaclass_validator
documentation:
Patch the internal validator function used during model construction to support modified metaclass.

Pydantic has a special guard which stops execution if a model defines field which shadows a basemodel interface.
For example, if someone would define a field named `__fields__` this would break code.

With the modified metaclass functionality, we are exposing the original fields as class attributes, and this
breaks this check.

This patcher method modifies the pydantic internals so that the check is retained, but it is not causing exception
when doing the normal field inheritance.

functions.name: openepd.patch_pydantic.patch_pydantic
documentation:
Modify Pydantic to support field attribute access via class.

Example: Field the_field in the model TheModel(BaseModel). Before this patch, one can do
 `TheModel().__fields__["the_field"]' to get field descriptor. After the fix, one can do TheModel.the_field.

 To disable, set env variable `OPENEPD_DISABLE_PYDANTIC_PATCH` to "1", "yes" or "true".

classes.name: openepd.model.validation.quantity.QuantityValidator

methods.name: openepd.model.validation.quantity.QuantityValidator.validate_same_dimensionality
documentation:
Validate that a given unit ('kg') has the same dimesnionality as provided dimensionality_unit ('g').

```
param unit

unit to validate, not quantity

param dimensionality_unit

unit to check against

raise

ValueError if dimensionality is different

return

None
```

methods.name: openepd.model.validation.quantity.QuantityValidator.validate_unit_correctness
documentation:
Validate the given string value against the given dimensionality.

Args:
    value: The value to validate, like "102.4 kg"
    dimensionality: The dimensionality to validate against, like "kg"
Returns:
    None if the value is valid, raises an error otherwise.
Raises:
    ValueError: If the value is not valid.

methods.name: openepd.model.validation.quantity.QuantityValidator.validate_quantity_greater_or_equal
documentation:
Validate the quantity is greater than minimal value.

Args:
    value: The value to validate, like "2.4 kg"
    min_value: The value to compare with, like "102.4 kg"
Returns:
    None if the value is valid, raises an error otherwise.
Raises:
    ValueError: If the value is not valid.

methods.name: openepd.model.validation.quantity.QuantityValidator.validate_quantity_less_or_equal
documentation:
Validate the quantity is less than minimal value.

Args:
    value: The value to validate, like "2.4 kg"
    max_value: The value to compare with, like "0.4 kg"
Returns:
    None if the value is valid, raises an error otherwise.
Raises:
    ValueError: If the value is not valid.

Interface for quantity validator.

The openEPD models are mapped using the simple types. Caller code should provide their own implementation of this
and set it with `set_unit_validator` function.
